title: Broken Cask Society - culinary adventure time (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hUQqvZGsp6A

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to one of his favourite fantasy niches — taverns and inns — with Broken Cask Society by Derek A. Kamal (art by Claudia Cangini), the follow-up to The Broken Cask. Where the original cast you as an innkeeper, here you play a member of a society that roams the land seeking out the finest inns, pubs and meaderies: a kind of fantasy Anthony Bourdain on a perpetual pub crawl.

Each session follows a tidy outline — prepare, journey, arrive, feast, rest. Characters (from playbooks like the Pilgrim, or built from scratch) run on body/mind/heart stats plus morale, gear, XP and a "mirth" currency that powers basic moves. A neat solo option lets you bring an intern to assist your tests. It's a cozy, simple journaling game that pairs beautifully with Madeleine Hale's Table Fables and Inns & Taverns guides.

He narrates a full playthrough: jigsaw Jenny and her intern Fallen Taja set out hunting a mythical mobile micro-pub called the Water Wagon, weathering creepy night-birds, windstorms and a poisonous flower, before feasting at the Buzzing Twig Inn — iron-fold ham, spicy chai, a regrettable Merlot, and a triumphant dragon-scale charcuterie. Charming, warm, and exactly his kind of low-stakes adventure.

----

Would a cozy "tour the inns and taverns" game scratch an itch for you — or do you need higher stakes than the next good meal?
